note: Typed relationships between protocols. NOT a hierarchy — tiers already handle priority. These edges are data + queryable via edges.py; they are NOT wired into the live matcher (prompt_process/triggers.json left untouched on purpose). Wiring them into activation is a later, human-reviewed step. v2 (2026-06-25): connected the 15 graph-orphans with true typed edges so the whole library is one coherent graph; extended the failure-handling spine (stop-trigger -> struggle-protocol -> error-recovery -> reflect); added intentional_leaves for genuinely standalone protocols so `edges.py orphans` only flags UNEXPECTED disconnection.

types.governs: A is a meta-protocol that decides when/whether to use B (the only hierarchical type; mostly mirrors tiers)
types.escalates_to: directed workflow: when A's condition recurs or fails, move to B
types.pairs_with: lateral: consult B alongside A (no up/down)
types.indexes: A is a catalog/reference that points at B

intentional_leaves_note: Protocols deliberately left unconnected because they have no true relationship to the runtime-discipline graph. medium-article is a specialized output/formatting protocol (Medium-ready prose); forcing an edge would be noise. `edges.py orphans` treats these as expected, not as coherence gaps.
